meta.title: Keeply vs Google Drive Version History — Out of the menu, into the timeline
meta.description: Google Drive hides version history three clicks deep, offers no visual diff, and caps retention. Keeply surfaces every save on a per-project timeline with side-by-side diffs — free forever.

hero.badge: 📘 Drive's version history is buried for a reason
hero.h1_html: Tired of hunting for File → Version history every time you want a past version?
hero.sub_html: Google Drive keeps versions, but they're buried behind a menu, capped at 30 days or 100 revisions, and impossible to diff visually. Keeply puts every save on a project timeline with side-by-side visual diff — and it's free forever for personal use.
hero.tagline: Free forever for personal use · Team $25/mo flat (coming soon).

what_happened.title: Version history you have to dig for — and that quietly expires
what_happened.body_html: Google Drive stores file revisions, but by default keeps them only for 30 days or 100 revisions, whichever comes first. You can manually mark individual revisions as "Keep forever" — if you remember, one file at a time. Opening that history means File → Version history → See version history, then clicking each revision to preview. There is no side-by-side diff — only a full-document preview of the picked version. For Workspace Business plans at $12–$18 per user per month, that is a lot of clicks and a lot of money for a feature most teams outgrow.
what_happened.source: Source: Google Drive Help — file versions and revision retention

pain_points.title: Version history hidden three clicks deep.
pain_points.body: File → Version history → See version history → click each revision to preview. You do this ten times a week and it never gets faster.

pain_points.title: No way to compare what actually changed.
pain_points.body: Drive shows you the old version as a preview — but you can't see what text, numbers, or pixels differ from the current file. You open both, tab between them, and guess.

pain_points.title: The 30-day / 100-revision cap.
pain_points.body: Drive quietly deletes old revisions unless you remember to mark each one "Keep forever." For active projects with daily edits, the cap hits within weeks.

pain_points.title: Offline? No version history at all.
pain_points.body: Versioning lives in Google's cloud. If you're on a plane, at a client site without Wi-Fi, or Drive is having an outage, the history is simply unreachable.

migration.title: Sync your Drive folder to local
migration.body: Install Google Drive for Desktop and let it mirror your working folders locally. Keeply needs real files on your disk to track versions — not web-only documents.

migration.title: Install Keeply and open the local Drive folder
migration.body: Download Keeply, open the synced project folder, click Start protecting. Every save from now on goes on the timeline with a description you write.

migration.title: Export your Google Docs to real file formats
migration.body: For Docs/Sheets/Slides you want to version properly, File → Download as .docx / .xlsx / .pptx. Keeply's visual diff works on these real files, not on Google's proprietary formats.

migration.title: Add a backup location you control
migration.body: In Keeply, add a NAS, USB drive, or GitHub repo as a backup target. Now your version history lives on storage you own — Google outages and retention caps don't reach it.

faq.q: Can I keep using Google Drive for sharing and still use Keeply?
faq.a: Yes, they solve different problems. Drive shares files with collaborators in real time; Keeply tracks the version history of your working files locally. Sync Drive to your desktop, point Keeply at the synced folder, and both run in parallel. Drive handles sharing, Keeply handles the history you actually need to retrieve.

faq.q: Does Keeply work with Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides?
faq.a: Only after you download them to real file formats (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx). Google's native Docs format lives only on Google's servers and Keeply never sees it. For documents you care about keeping long-term, File → Download regularly — Keeply then versions those files with full visual diff support.

faq.q: Why is Drive's version history considered limited?
faq.a: Three reasons. Retention defaults to 30 days or 100 revisions, whichever hits first. There is no side-by-side diff — only a preview of each picked version. And on non-Google file types the history is a list of opaque snapshots without descriptions. Keeply addresses all three: unlimited retention, real visual diff, and required save notes.

faq.q: Do I have to stop paying for Workspace?
faq.a: Not at all. Keep Workspace for email, shared drives, and Meet — those are worth the money. Use Keeply's free tier to replace the version history part specifically. You stop paying per seat for a version feature you outgrow in a month, without losing any of the collaboration value Workspace provides.

Keeply works fully offline — versions get tracked to your local disk the instant you save, regardless of internet. When you reconnect, any configured backup locations (NAS, GitHub, Gitea) sync automatically. Drive, by contrast, needs a live connection to read or write version history, which breaks the moment you board a plane.

faq.q: Where are my versions actually stored?
faq.a: On your own storage. Keeply writes version data to a hidden folder inside your project on your local drive, then optionally mirrors it to any backup location you pick: office NAS (SMB), USB drive, self-hosted Gitea, or a private GitHub repo (up to three destinations). Keeply runs no server that holds your project data. Cancel anytime; files and history stay with you.

meta.title: Keeply vs Google Drive 版本歷史 — 從選單深處搬到時間軸
meta.description: Google Drive 的版本歷史藏在三層選單裡，沒有視覺比對，還有保留期限。Keeply 把每次存檔放在專案時間軸上，附並排比對——個人永久免費。

hero.badge: 📘 Drive 的版本歷史藏得很深是有原因的
hero.h1_html: 每次要找舊版本都要翻 「檔案 → 版本記錄」？
hero.sub_html: Google Drive 有版本記錄，但藏在選單深處、預設只留 30 天或 100 個版本、沒辦法做視覺比對。Keeply 把每次存檔攤開在專案時間軸上，附並排視覺比對——個人永久免費。
hero.tagline: 個人永久免費 · Team $25/月 不按人頭（Coming Soon）。

what_happened.title: 要挖才找得到、而且會默默過期的版本歷史
what_happened.body_html: Google Drive 會存檔案版本，但預設只保留 30 天或 100 個版本，哪個先到就先砍。你可以手動把個別版本標記為「永久保留」——前提是你記得，而且一個檔案一個檔案來。要打開歷史要走「檔案 → 版本記錄 → 查看版本記錄」，然後一個一個點開預覽。沒有並排比對——只能看整份文件的某個版本。付 Workspace Business 每人每月 $12–$18 美金，買到的是這樣的功能，點擊多、錢也多。
what_happened.source: 資料來源：Google Drive 說明 — 檔案版本與版本保留

pain_points.title: 版本歷史藏在三層選單後面。
pain_points.body: 「檔案 → 版本記錄 → 查看版本記錄 → 一個一個點開看」。一週做十次，永遠不會變快。

pain_points.title: 沒辦法比對到底改了什麼。
pain_points.body: Drive 讓你看舊版本的預覽，但看不出哪段文字、哪個數字、哪個像素跟現在的檔案不一樣。只能兩個都開、切換分頁、用猜的。

pain_points.title: 30 天 / 100 版本上限。
pain_points.body: Drive 會默默刪掉舊版本，除非你記得把每一個都標記為「永久保留」。活躍專案天天改，幾週就撞牆。

pain_points.title: 離線？版本歷史完全消失。
pain_points.body: 版本資料在 Google 雲端。飛機上、客戶端沒 Wi-Fi、Drive 掛掉的時候，歷史就是完全拿不到。

migration.title: 把 Drive 資料夾同步到本機
migration.body: 安裝 Google Drive for Desktop，讓它把工作資料夾鏡像到本機。Keeply 需要磁碟上的真實檔案才能管版本——線上專用的文件不行。

migration.title: 安裝 Keeply，打開本機的 Drive 資料夾
migration.body: 下載 Keeply，開啟同步下來的專案資料夾，點「開始保護」。從此每次存檔都進時間軸，附上你寫的附註。

migration.title: 把 Google 文件匯出為真實格式
migration.body: 想好好管版本的 Docs / Sheets / Slides，用「檔案 → 下載」存成 .docx / .xlsx / .pptx。Keeply 的視覺比對對這些真實檔案有效，對 Google 的專有格式無效。

migration.title: 新增一個你掌握的備份位置
migration.body: 在 Keeply 裡新增 NAS、隨身碟、或 GitHub repo 作為備份目標。版本歷史從此放在你擁有的儲存空間——Google 掛了、上限到了都影響不到它。

faq.q: 可以繼續用 Google Drive 分享檔案、同時用 Keeply 嗎？
faq.a: 可以，它們解決不同問題。Drive 即時分享檔案給協作者；Keeply 在本機追蹤工作檔案的版本歷史。把 Drive 同步到桌面，Keeply 指向同步下來的資料夾，兩者平行運作。分享交給 Drive，歷史交給 Keeply。

faq.q: Keeply 支援 Google Docs、Sheets、Slides 嗎？
faq.a: 要先下載成真實檔案格式（.docx / .xlsx / .pptx）才行。Google 原生的 Docs 格式只存在 Google 伺服器上，Keeply 看不到。想長期保存的文件記得定期「檔案 → 下載」——Keeply 會接手管版本並提供視覺比對。

faq.q: 為什麼說 Drive 的版本歷史有限？
faq.a: 三個理由。預設保留 30 天或 100 個版本，先到先刪。沒有並排比對——只能預覽某個版本。非 Google 格式的歷史就是一堆沒附註的不透明快照。Keeply 三個都解：無限保留、真正的視覺比對、每次存檔要你寫附註。

faq.q: 我需要停掉 Workspace 嗎？
faq.a: 完全不用。Workspace 的 Email、共用雲端硬碟、Meet 還是值得的錢。用 Keeply 免費版換掉版本歷史那一塊就好。不再為一個用一個月就嫌小的功能每人每月付錢，也完全不影響 Workspace 的協作價值。
Keeply 完全離線可用——存檔瞬間就寫進本機磁碟，不管有沒有網路。連線恢復後，你設定的備份位置（NAS、GitHub、Gitea）會自動同步。Drive 剛好相反，讀寫版本歷史都要連線，一上飛機就掛。

faq.q: 我的版本實際存在哪裡？
faq.a: 放在你自己的儲存空間。Keeply 把版本資料寫進你專案裡的隱藏資料夾（本機磁碟），再選擇性鏡像到你挑的備份位置：辦公室 NAS（SMB）、隨身碟、自架 Gitea、或私有 GitHub repo（最多三個目的地）。Keeply 自家伺服器完全不存你的專案資料。隨時不用，檔案和歷史都留著。
